title: svg-illustration widget params
description: A static SVG figure inside standard widget chrome. No driving script, no interactive controls — the widget is a pure illustration that lives inside the registry for metadata (family / dimension) and portability across alternate frontends. Optional `caption` and `footer` describe the after-SVG text blocks (a `<div class="small">` paragraph or a `<div class="readout">` with optional id + inline style). The SVG's inner HTML is carried verbatim as `svgInner` — typically just a `<title>` element plus any hand-drawn static content. A companion `widget-script` block may still sit next to this widget in `content/<topic>.json` — it is rendered untouched by the topic renderer, so illustrations whose pixels are produced by a neighbouring load-time script can migrate to this widget without breaking.

widgetId.description: DOM id on the outer `<div class="widget">`.

viewBox.description: `viewBox` attribute on the `<svg>`. Restricted to the `0 0 W H` shape the corpus uses — the renderer derives the `width` and `height` attributes from W and H.

title.description: Widget title. Rendered in both the `.ttl` header and as the inner text of the SVG's `<title>` element (for accessibility).

hint.description: Short line rendered in the `.hint` slot of the header. Optional; when omitted no hint div is emitted.

svgInner.description: ARTIFACT. Raw inner HTML of the SVG, excluding the inner `<title>` element (which the renderer emits automatically from `title`). Usually an empty string — the static-svg corpus overwhelmingly carries only the title element inside the SVG and lets a sibling `widget-script` block draw decoration at load time. Non-empty values preserve hand-drawn static SVG markup verbatim.

afterSvg.description:
An after-SVG text block. `kind: 'small'` emits `
{content}
`; `kind: 'readout'` emits `
{content}
`. `content` is raw HTML (escape rules match the on-page bytes — e.g. `<` in prose stays encoded).

style.description: ARTIFACT. Raw inline-style attribute value (e.g. `font-size:.85rem`). Several absorbed widgets set bespoke readout styling that the shared CSS doesn't cover.

content.description: Raw HTML content inside the div. Defaults to the empty string when omitted.
